annotations.label: Central limit theorem (informal)

anchor.snippet: the distribution of a normalized version of the sample mean converges to a standard normal distribution

annotations.note: The informal statement matches Mathlib's 1D i.i.d. CLT giving convergence in distribution of the standardized sum to a Gaussian.

annotations.label: CLT statistical statement

anchor.snippet: the CLT can be stated as

annotations.note: The standard CLT statement is formalized via `tendstoInDistribution_inv_sqrt_mul_sum_sub` to `gaussianReal`.

annotations.label: de Moivre–Laplace theorem

anchor.snippet: the normal distribution may be used as an approximation to the binomial distribution

annotations.note: The binomial special case is not stated separately; it is subsumed by the general i.i.d. CLT.

annotations.label: Law of large numbers (sample average)

anchor.snippet: the sample average converges almost surely

annotations.note: Almost-sure convergence of the sample average is Mathlib's strong law of large numbers.

annotations.label: Classical CLT (uniform convergence of cdfs)

anchor.snippet: convergence in distribution means that the cumulative distribution functions

annotations.note: Mathlib's CLT is stated in the equivalent form of convergence in distribution (weak convergence of laws).

annotations.label: Lindeberg–Lévy CLT
annotations.note: Lindeberg–Lévy is exactly what `tendstoInDistribution_inv_sqrt_mul_sum_sub` states.

annotations.label: Lyapunov CLT
annotations.note: No Lyapunov condition or Lyapunov CLT appears in Mathlib (loogle returns 0 hits for `Lyapunov`).

annotations.label: Lyapunov implies Lindeberg

anchor.snippet: If a sequence of random variables satisfies Lyapunov's condition, then it also satisfies Lindeberg's condition

annotations.note: Neither the Lyapunov nor the Lindeberg condition is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lindeberg condition

anchor.snippet: the Lyapunov condition can be replaced with the following weaker one (from Lindeberg in 1920)

annotations.note: Loogle finds no declaration whose name contains `Lindeberg`.

annotations.label: Lindeberg–Feller CLT

anchor.snippet: the distribution of the standardized sums

annotations.note: The Lindeberg–Feller CLT for non-i.i.d. arrays is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Robbins CLT
annotations.note: Robbins' CLT for random sums is not formalized.

annotations.label: Multidimensional CLT

anchor.snippet: The multidimensional central limit theorem states that when scaled, sums converge to a multivariate normal distribution

annotations.note: Mathlib's CLT is one-dimensional only; no multidimensional CLT is proven though `multivariateGaussian` is defined.

annotations.label: Berry–Esseen type bound for multivariate CLT
annotations.note: No Berry–Esseen bound (univariate or multivariate) is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cramér–Wold theorem

anchor.snippet: The multivariate central limit theorem can be proved using the Cramér–Wold theorem

annotations.note: The Cramér–Wold device is not formalized (loogle returns 0 hits for `CramerWold`).

annotations.label: Generalized central limit theorem

anchor.value: Statement of GCLT — A non-degenerate ran

annotations.note: Stable distributions and the generalized CLT are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Strong mixing (α-mixing)

anchor.snippet: strong mixing (also called α-mixing) defined by

annotations.note: No α-mixing coefficient is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: CLT under strong mixing
annotations.note: No mixing-based CLT is formalized.

annotations.label: Martingale difference CLT

anchor.snippet: Let a martingale

annotations.note: Mathlib has martingale theory but no martingale CLT (loogle finds no `centralLimit`/martingale match).

annotations.label: Proof of classical CLT via characteristic functions

anchor.snippet: The central limit theorem has a proof using characteristic functions

annotations.note: Mathlib's proof goes via characteristic functions with this key pointwise limit and Lévy's continuity theorem.

annotations.label: Lévy's continuity theorem

anchor.snippet: implies through Lévy's continuity theorem that the distribution

annotations.note: Lévy's continuity theorem is formalized as the equivalence between weak convergence and pointwise convergence of characteristic functions.

annotations.label: Berry–Esseen rate of convergence

anchor.snippet: the speed of convergence is at least on the order of

annotations.note: Berry–Esseen rate bounds are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Monotone entropy convergence

anchor.snippet: The convergence to the normal distribution is monotonic

annotations.note: Monotonic entropy convergence to the normal is not formalized.

annotations.label: Law of the iterated logarithm

anchor.section: Relation to the law of large numbers
anchor.snippet: The law of the iterated logarithm specifies what is happening

annotations.note: The law of the iterated logarithm is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stable distribution

anchor.section: Relation to the law of large numbers
anchor.snippet: Distributions Ξ which can arise in this way are called stable

annotations.note: Stable distributions are not defined in Mathlib (only Cauchy and Gaussian are present).

annotations.label: Local limit theorem (density convolution)

anchor.snippet: the convolution of a number of density functions tends to the normal density

annotations.note: No local limit theorem (density-level convergence) appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: CLT restated via characteristic functions

anchor.snippet: the product of the characteristic functions of a number of density functions becomes close to the characteristic function of the normal density

annotations.note: Mathlib expresses the characteristic function of the normalized sum as a power and shows it converges to the Gaussian char function.

annotations.label: Variance lemma for stationary sequences
annotations.note: No stationary-sequence variance lemma exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiplicative CLT (Gibrat's law)

anchor.snippet: when the logarithm of a product of random variables that take only positive values approaches a normal distribution, the product itself approaches a log-normal distribution

annotations.note: No log-normal distribution or multiplicative CLT is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: CLT for convex bodies (total variation)
annotations.note: Klartag's CLT for convex bodies is not formalized.

annotations.label: Berry–Esseen result for log-concave densities
annotations.note: No Berry–Esseen-type result for log-concave densities is formalized.

annotations.label: Salem–Zygmund theorem
annotations.note: The Salem–Zygmund theorem on lacunary trigonometric series is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: CLT for Gaussian polytope area
annotations.note: No CLT for random polytope area/volume is formalized.

annotations.label: CLT for linear functions of orthogonal matrices
annotations.note: No CLT for linear functionals of Haar-random orthogonal matrices is formalized.

annotations.label: Subsequence CLT
annotations.note: No subsequence CLT exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: CLT on a crystal lattice

anchor.section: Random walk on a crystal lattice
anchor.snippet: The central limit theorem may be established for the simple random walk on a crystal lattice

annotations.note: Crystal lattices and CLTs for random walks on them are not formalized.

annotations.label: Rolling many dice

anchor.snippet: A simple example of the central limit theorem is rolling many identical, unbiased dice

annotations.note: The dice-rolling illustration is not formalized as a standalone example in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regression error terms

anchor.snippet: even if the individual error terms are not normally distributed, by the central limit theorem their sum can be well approximated by a normal distribution

annotations.note: The CLT-for-regression-errors application is not formalized.

annotations.label: Convergence in distribution

anchor.snippet: convergence in distribution means that the cumulative distribution functions

annotations.note: Weak convergence of probability measures (convergence in distribution) is formalized via the topology on `ProbabilityMeasure`.

annotations.label: Multivariate normal (Gaussian) distribution

anchor.snippet: sums converge to a multivariate normal distribution

annotations.note: Multivariate Gaussian distributions are defined in Mathlib though no multidimensional CLT targets them.

annotations.label: Characteristic function of a random variable

anchor.snippet: The characteristic function of

annotations.note: The characteristic function E[e^{itX}] is formalized as `MeasureTheory.charFun` (module corrected to `…CharacteristicFunction.Basic`).
